programs.name: Golden Buckeye Program
programs.what_it_does: The Golden Buckeye Program connects Ohio residents with businesses offering discounts, waived fees, free products, and services. Ohio residents are automatically enrolled at age 60 and receive a card in the mail.[1][2]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 60+ or aged 18-59 with a disability.[1][2]
programs.whats_unique: State-run discount card program automatically enrolling all Ohio seniors at 60, linking them to local business perks not available nationwide.[1][2]
programs.benefit_value: Discounts, waived fees, free products/services from participating businesses; value varies by usage.

programs.name: Healthy Aging Grants
programs.what_it_does: Provides $40 million in state funding to 85 Ohio counties for local aging services like food, housing assistance, and internet access. Counties receive a base $100,000 plus more based on non-Medicaid seniors 60+.[3][8]
programs.who_its_for: Ohioans aged 60+ via county programs; targets healthy aging and independence.[3]
programs.whats_unique: Ohio-specific $40M state grant initiative (launched 2023 via Gov. DeWine's budget) flexibly funding local priorities beyond federal programs.[3][8]
programs.benefit_value: $40M total statewide; per county $100K base + population-based supplement.

programs.name: Senior Citizen Tax Credit
programs.what_it_does: Offers an income tax credit on retirement and pension income for qualifying seniors. Maximum benefit is $200 with income limits.[4]
programs.who_its_for: Ohio residents aged 65+ with modified AGI under $100,000.[4]
programs.whats_unique: Ohio state income tax credit specifically for senior retirement/pension income, distinct from federal taxes.[4]

programs.name: Elderly Services Program (ESP)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers in-home services like personal care, housekeeping, meals, and transportation to promote independence. Funded by county tax levies to prevent nursing home placement.[5]
programs.who_its_for: Residents 60+ (or 65+ in some counties) needing ADL/IADL help, ineligible for Medicaid/Medicare/insurance.[5]
programs.whats_unique: County tax levy-funded home care in southwest Ohio (Butler, Clinton, Hamilton, Warren), expanding family care beyond federal waivers.[5]
programs.benefit_value: In-home services including personal care, meals, transport; value based on assessed needs.

programs.name: Home Repair Program
programs.what_it_does: State-funded initiative for minor home repairs and accessibility modifications. Helps older adults maintain safe housing.[6]
programs.whats_unique: Ohio Department of Aging-funded program specifically for senior home repairs/modifications, not universally available across states.[6]
programs.benefit_value: Minor repairs and modifications; specific amounts vary by need.

programs.name: Franklin County Senior Services Levy Programs (Senior Options)
programs.what_it_does: Funds services like adult day care, respite, case management, meals, homemaker help via property tax levy. Serves as one-stop access for independence.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Franklin County residents aged 60+ with functional impairments.[7]
programs.whats_unique: County-specific property tax levy (since 1992) funding comprehensive senior options, unique to local voter-approved structure.[7]
programs.benefit_value: Varied services (day care, meals, respite); funded by ongoing county levy.
